changes_from_iteration_1: Removed all GPT Agent / ChatGPT routing references (description, Phase 1.5, Inspector template, Self-Improvement)
Added 'Special Rule for Multi-Option Deliverables': must pick a primary recommendation with reasoning
Added explicit caveman example showing synthesis sections must stay caveman (vs slip into normal prose)
Inspector template: removed Routing line, added optional 'Primary pick' line for WRITING/STRATEGY

delta_v2_vs_v1.duration_seconds: +8.4s (slower, mostly because eval-0 produced a more thorough fix; not a concerning regression)

analyst_observations: All assertions pass on iteration 2. The two new requirements (no GPT mention, primary pick) both held across all three task types.
Caveman style now holds even through synthesis sections (research eval) — the explicit before/after example in the skill carried through.
Writing eval picked Version B with audience-grounded reasoning ('waitlist already self-selected, FOMO has weaker bite') — exactly the kind of recommendation the user asked for.
Coding eval slowed by ~20s. Likely cause: more thorough fix (FORCE RLS + belt-and-braces policy). Trade-off: deeper output. Acceptable.
Recommendation: ship this version. Optional iteration 3 only if user wants to compress coding response or further tighten caveman in long technical sections.
